{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimizer and schedule already improved early learning, so the next likely bottleneck is model capacity. A modest width increase plus one extra residual block keeps the architecture simple and should improve representation quality within the same 256-step budget without changing the training contract.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        hidden_channels = channels * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, hidden_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(hidden_channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(hidden_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(hidden_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(hidden_channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","old":"        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current model improved a lot from added capacity, so the next high-value change is a more ResNet-like stage transition. Adding a stride-2 channel expansion uses the existing CHANNEL_MULT, increases representational power, and reduces spatial cost so the extra depth should fit the fixed 256-step budget better than only stacking more same-resolution blocks.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current model and schedule are already using the fixed step budget effectively, so a low-complexity robustness change is the next clean test. Label smoothing is cheap, stable, and often improves CIFAR validation loss by reducing overconfident predictions without changing the optimizer or architecture.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size lets the model see substantially more training examples within the same number of optimizer updates. Raising the learning rate modestly alongside the larger batch preserves update scale and is a simple way to test whether the current bottleneck is data coverage per step rather than model capacity.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last jump suggests the current regime was underpowered in effective optimization rather than overfitting. With the larger batch and OneCycle schedule now in place, a modest additional learning-rate increase is a clean next probe that can improve progress per step without adding architectural complexity or more wall-time pressure from a larger model.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher learning-rate probe slightly regressed, so the next clean step is to return to the stronger 3e-3 setting and refine regularization. With the larger batch already improving data coverage, reducing label smoothing from 0.1 to 0.05 may preserve its calibration benefit while reducing underconfidence and improving validation cross-entropy.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization settings are now strong and incremental regularization tweaks are yielding only small gains, which suggests capacity may be the next limiter. Increasing the second-stage width and classifier size is a compact way to test more representational power without restructuring the training loop or changing the proven batch/schedule regime.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The widened second stage delivered a large gain, which makes mild regularization tuning a good next probe. A somewhat stronger AdamW weight decay is a simple way to control the larger model’s capacity and often improves CIFAR validation loss without disturbing the batch-size and scheduler settings that are already working well.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current model is larger and already benefiting from stronger weight decay, so a small amount of dropout is a sensible next regularization probe. Setting a modest dropout rate should test whether the improved capacity can generalize better without materially changing the successful optimizer, schedule, or batch configuration.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        pct_start=0.08,","old":"        pct_start=0.15,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Dropout was clearly too harmful for this short-budget regime, so the next step is to restore the stronger no-dropout configuration and reallocate more of the 256 steps to high learning rates. Shortening the OneCycle warmup should better exploit the fixed step budget now that the larger batch and wider second stage are already stable.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        pct_start=0.15,","old":"        pct_start=0.08,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        final_div_factor=30.0,","old":"        final_div_factor=100.0,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The shorter warmup regressed, so the next step should restore the stronger schedule shape while testing a less aggressive late-stage decay. With only 256 optimizer steps, annealing all the way down to 1/100 of the peak learning rate may be too conservative for this larger model, and a higher ending rate can preserve useful optimization pressure near the end of training.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 18","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The schedule and regularization are now fairly well tuned, and recent gains from architectural capacity have been stronger than most optimizer-only tweaks. A small increase in the base width should raise both stage capacities without changing the successful overall design, making it a reasonable next probe for additional validation-loss improvement.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last width increase produced a large improvement, which suggests the current bottleneck may still be representational capacity rather than optimization. Increasing only the classifier hidden width is a relatively cheap capacity test compared with widening the convolutional trunk further, so it preserves most of the successful setup while adding little runtime risk.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.02)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The larger classifier head regressed, so the next step should restore the stronger hidden size and refine the remaining regularization. With the widened convolutional trunk already doing most of the work, reducing label smoothing from 0.05 to 0.02 may preserve some calibration benefit while letting the model fit the cleaner CIFAR-10 labels more sharply.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 18","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Recent gains have come most reliably from increasing convolutional trunk capacity, while several optimizer and head-only tweaks were weaker or negative. A small additional increase in base width is the cleanest next probe because it scales both stages of the network while preserving the now-strong schedule, batch size, weight decay, and label-smoothing settings.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 18","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The additional width regressed, so the stronger trunk size should be restored. With that rollback in place, a slightly smaller batch is a useful next probe because it still covers more than one epoch within 256 steps while adding a bit more gradient noise, which can improve generalization in this short-budget regime without changing the proven optimizer or schedule.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.02)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The smaller batch regressed, so the stronger 256-example setting should be restored. With clean labels and a much stronger convolutional trunk than the earlier baseline, it is now reasonable to test removing label smoothing entirely to see whether sharper targets improve validation cross-entropy more than the small amount of remaining regularization.","secondary_modes":["micro","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 4e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.02)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The no-smoothing probe regressed, so the stronger 0.02 label smoothing should be restored. For the final step, a modest additional increase in AdamW weight decay is a reasonable low-risk attempt to further regularize the widened trunk, since earlier weight-decay tuning helped and this change preserves the architecture and schedule that produced the best results so far.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}
